mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns no more often than the unconditional base rate over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment falls while payrolls expand, labor income expectations improve and equity investors mark up cyclically sensitive cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.3pp over 3 months and PAYEMS rises over the same 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have lower mean 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward test windows.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound, rising consumer prices reduce real short rates because nominal policy rates are pinned, supporting equity valuation multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.5 and CPIAUCSL rises more than 1.0% over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if this trigger is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences or if the conditional mean return is not above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production over three months signals weakening real activity and margin pressure, causing investors to lower earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO 3-month contractions are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently depressed housing starts signal weak household formation and construction demand, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST remains below 600 for 3 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below 600 for three months is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more often than the unconditional positive-return frequency.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate from its prior 6-month level tightens discount rates and signals less policy accommodation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 0.25pp versus its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if federal funds tightening triggers have non-negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns and a hit rate below 50% for negative returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of real GDP growth after a downturn identify sustained aggregate demand recovery, raising expected corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth is positive for 2 consecutive reported quarters
failure_condition: Reject if the two-quarter GDPC1 growth trigger does not improve 60-day S&P 500 return hit rate by at least 5 percentage points versus the unconditional hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment remains high while the policy rate is pinned near zero, weak labor slack prolongs monetary accommodation, supporting equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is above 8.5 and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-UNRATE low-FEDFUNDS condition is followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of walk-forward triggered observations.
